home.new_project.next_steps_desc: ⟦We'll create your project, open the editor, and point you to beginner-friendly buttons for adding your first mob, block, or item.⟧

home.new_project.storage_browser_desc: ⟦Quick try-it option. The project stays only in this browser on this device, so clearing browser data or switching browsers can remove it.⟧
home.new_project.storage_custom_desc: ⟦Pick a folder yourself if you want the project somewhere specific.⟧
home.new_project.storage_device_desc: ⟦Saved in a folder you choose. Your project stays safe after you close the browser — the best choice for almost every beginner project.⟧
home.new_project.storage_documents_desc: ⟦Automatically saved in Documents/Minecraft Creator Tools. A good default for most projects.⟧
home.new_project.storage_summary_documen: ⟦We'll create a new project folder in Documents/Minecraft Creator Tools and open it right away.⟧
home.new_project.storage_summary_browser: ⟦We'll open the project right away, but it will stay only in this browser unless you export or move it later.⟧

home.project_grid.advanced_description: ⟦You can ignore this section for now. Come back when you want commands, search, or code examples.⟧
home.project_grid.snippets_description: ⟦When you're ready to go further, browse code examples to learn how Minecraft features work and copy ideas into your project.⟧
home.project_grid.starters_description: ⟦New here? Start with Add-On Starter for the simplest first project, or pick another template if it matches what you want to build.⟧
home.project_grid.templates_offline_mess: ⟦The template gallery may be offline or blocked. Refresh to try again, or switch to offline templates if you're working without a connection.⟧

home.getting_started.title: ⟦Create your first mob in 2 minutes!⟧
home.getting_started.description: ⟦An add-on lets you create new mobs, items, blocks, and more for Minecraft. Pick an item below and start building — no coding required.⟧
home.addon_overview.title: ⟦What is an Add-On?⟧
home.addon_overview.description: ⟦An add-on is a content pack that adds new things to Minecraft — like custom mobs, blocks, and items. It's made of two parts: a behavior pack (how things work) and a resource pack (how things look). Minecraft Creator Tools helps you build both.⟧
gallery.addonStarter.title: ⟦Add-On Starter⟧
gallery.addonStarter.description: ⟦Best first project — start with an empty add-on and add your own mob, block, or item.⟧
gallery.tsStarter.title: ⟦Code Starter (TypeScript)⟧
gallery.tsStarter.description: ⟦Best if you want to write code. Starts with a simple TypeScript setup for script-based add-ons.⟧
gallery.addonFull.title: ⟦Full Add-On⟧
gallery.addonFull.description: ⟦A ready-made example add-on with sample mobs you can inspect, play with, and change.⟧
gallery.editor-basics.title: ⟦Minecraft Editor Tools⟧
gallery.editor-basics.description: ⟦Shows how to make tools for Minecraft Editor. Best after you've tried a regular add-on.⟧
gallery.dlStarter.title: ⟦Vibrant Visuals⟧
gallery.dlStarter.description: ⟦Try Vibrant Visuals with a simple starter pack you can tweak right away.⟧

project_editor.entity.loading_error: ⟦Unable to load mob definition. The data may not be available.⟧
project_editor.entity.tooltip_visuals: ⟦Visuals — Change how your mob looks (textures, models)⟧
project_editor.entity.tooltip_spawn: ⟦Spawn — Where and when your mob appears in the world⟧
project_editor.entity.tooltip_loot: ⟦Loot — Items dropped when your mob is defeated⟧
project_editor.entity.states_hint: ⟦Use base state for most edits. Advanced states are optional per-variant overrides.⟧
project_editor.entity.states_guide: ⟦Start with Default (base state). Enable advanced states only if you need different behaviors for Baby, Adult, or special variants.⟧
project_editor.entity.select_state_desc: ⟦Choose a state from the list on the left to see its behaviors and properties. Each state represents a variant (like Baby or Adult) that can change how your mob behaves.⟧
project_editor.entity.no_action_desc: ⟦Actions trigger entity behavior changes when events fire — for example, growing up or becoming angry. Select an action from the list on the left, or click Add Action above to create one.⟧
project_editor.entity.no_spawn_rules_des: ⟦Spawn rules control where and when your mob appears in the world. Add spawn rules to make your mob spawn naturally in Minecraft.⟧
project_editor.entity.no_loot_desc: ⟦Loot is what items your mob drops when defeated by a player. Common examples include raw meat (for animals), bones (for skeletons), or rare items (for bosses).⟧
project_editor.entity.diagram_help: ⟦This diagram shows how your mob makes decisions. Each box is a behavior group, and arrows show transitions between them.⟧
project_editor.entity.diagram_descriptio: ⟦This diagram shows how your mob makes decisions. Each box is a behavior group, and arrows show transitions between them.⟧
project_editor.entity.select_state_hint: ⟦Choose a state from the list on the left to see its behaviors and properties. Each state represents a variant (like Baby or Adult) that can change how your mob behaves.⟧
project_editor.entity.states_guidance: ⟦Default (base state) applies to this mob. Add states if you need different components for variants like baby, tamed, or saddled.⟧
project_editor.entity.loot_description: ⟦Loot is what items your mob drops when defeated by a player. Common examples include raw meat (for animals), bones (for skeletons), or rare items (for bosses).⟧
project_editor.entity.spawn_description: ⟦Spawn rules control where and when your mob appears in the world. Add spawn rules to make your mob spawn naturally in Minecraft.⟧

project_editor.item_comp.empty_state: ⟦No components added yet. Components define what your item does — like how much damage a sword deals or how much hunger food restores. Click the + button to add one.⟧

project_editor.block_overview.no_states: ⟦No states defined. States let your block change — like a door that opens and closes, or a lamp that turns on and off. Most simple blocks don't need states.⟧

project_item_type.tooltip.blockTypes: ⟦Definitions for blocks, materials, and terrain behavior.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.configDev: ⟦Configuration and metadata files used by tools and Minecraft to load the pack.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.design: ⟦Planning assets and placeholders used to prototype ideas.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.entityTypes: ⟦Definitions for mobs and NPCs, including behavior and spawn rules.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.itemTypes: ⟦Definitions for custom items, loot, recipes, and trading.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.modelsAnimatio: ⟦3D models, animations, and render controllers for visuals.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.scriptsLogic: ⟦Scripts and command logic that drive gameplay behavior.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.texturesAudio: ⟦Textures, images, particles, and audio used by the pack.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.uiUx: ⟦UI layouts, localization strings, and UI textures.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.vibrantVisuals: ⟦Lighting, PBR, and Vibrant Visuals rendering settings.⟧
project_item_type.tooltip.worldWorldgen: ⟦World data, biomes, features, and structure generation settings.⟧
home.template.desc_addonStarter: ⟦Start with a blank add-on and build your own ideas step by step.⟧
home.template.desc_tsStarter: ⟦Start a coding project with example files so you can add game logic quickly.⟧
home.template.desc_addonFull: ⟦Open a complete sample add-on with mobs you can explore and edit.⟧
home.template.desc_editor-basics: ⟦Start an Editor add-on project with simple examples for learning.⟧
home.template.desc_dlStarter: ⟦Try Vibrant Visuals with a simple starter pack you can tweak right away.⟧
home.template.desc_scriptBox: ⟦Use a small sandbox project to test scripts and see what they do.⟧
home.template.desc_editor-scriptBox: ⟦Start a basic Editor script project for quick experiments.⟧
home.template.desc_cottaGame: ⟦Play a complete sample game built with TypeScript — great for learning game logic.⟧
home.template.desc_registeringExampleCus: ⟦See how to add custom behavior to blocks and items using components.⟧
home.template.desc_editor-multi: ⟦Explore a full-featured Editor add-on with advanced integration examples.⟧
viewer.rotate_hint: ⟦Use mouse to rotate, scroll to zoom⟧
project_editor.minecraft.deploy_locally: ⟦Deploy this project to Minecraft hosted locally⟧
project_editor.minecraft.deploy_remotely: ⟦Deploy this project to Minecraft hosted remotely⟧
project_editor.minecraft.settings_applie: ⟦Settings will be applied the next time the server starts.⟧
world_settings.transient_world_tooltip: ⟦When enabled, world data is not backed up and each deployment starts fresh. Useful for development.⟧
share_project.error_header: ⟦Could not generate links with changed content.⟧
share_project.url_too_long: ⟦URL is too long ({length} chars) for a QR code (over 4200 characters).⟧
share_project.content_warning: ⟦This changed content is directly included within the links above. By sharing "with Changes" links, you are sharing this content:⟧
integrate_item.desc_mcworld: ⟦An exported world package. Its contents will be extracted into your project.⟧
integrate_item.desc_mcproject: ⟦A creator project package. Its contents will be merged into your project.⟧
integrate_item.desc_template: ⟦A world template package with starter content.⟧
integrate_item.desc_addon: ⟦An add-on with behavior and/or resource packs.⟧
integrate_item.desc_archive: ⟦A zip archive. Its contents will be extracted into your project.⟧
integrate_item.desc_sound: ⟦An audio file that will be added to your project's sounds.⟧
integrate_item.desc_structure: ⟦A Minecraft structure file used for world generation or structure blocks.⟧
integrate_item.desc_snbt: ⟦An SNBT structure file that will be converted and added to your project.⟧
integrate_item.desc_bbmodel: ⟦A Blockbench 3D model. Its geometry, animations, and textures will be imported.⟧
integrate_item.desc_json: ⟦A JSON data file — could be an entity, block, item definition, or other content.⟧
integrate_item.desc_image: ⟦An image file that can be used as a texture in your project.⟧
integrate_item.desc_script: ⟦A script file for game logic and add-on behavior.⟧
integrate_item.desc_default: ⟦This file will be added to your project.⟧
